content:
# Gas Condensate

**Summary**: A reservoir containing both gas and liquid hydrocarbons (condensate).

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2012-baarimah|BAARIMAH, 2012]] studied a gas-condensate reservoir to analyze the effects of condensate blockage.

- [[2001-humoud|Humoud, 2001]] analyzed gas-condensate systems to develop more accurate prediction models for dewpoint pressure.

## Scientific Context

- [[2012-baarimah|BAARIMAH, 2012]] Requires specific well designs to manage condensate blockage.

- [[2001-humoud|Humoud, 2001]] analyzed gas-condensate systems to develop more accurate prediction models for dewpoint pressure.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2012-baarimah|BAARIMAH, 2012]] Analyzed via simulation of oil saturation distribution.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Condensate blockage near the wellbore can cause significant loss in deliverability for low-to-moderate permeability reservoirs. ([[2012-baarimah|BAARIMAH, 2012, p. 101]])


- The study focused on 74 data sets of gas-condensate systems from the Middle East region. ([[2001-humoud|Humoud, 2001, p. 16]])
